docket_entries.description: DECLARATION OF MARTIN L. SHIVES IN SUPPORT OF RESPONSE TO AMENDED PETITION (TRANSACTION ID # 76194726) FILED BY RESPONDENT BERKSHIRE HATHAWAY LIFE INSURANCE COMPANY OF NEBRASKA

LAW AND MOTION 302, HEARING ON NOTICE OF HEARING ON FIRST AMENDED PETITION FOR APPROVAL OF TRANSFER OF STRUCTURED SETTLEMENT PAYMENT RIGHTS. THE MATTER IS ARGUED. AT THE CONCLUSION OF ARGUMENT, THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING. PETITIONER TBT SERVICES, LLC'S FIRST AMENDED PETITION FOR APPROVAL OF TRANSFER OF STRUCTURED SETTLEMENT PAYMENT RIGHTS IS DENIED. SEE MINI MINUTES FOR COMPLETE RULING. JUDGE: JOSEPH M. QUINN. CLERK: ROBERT WOODS. NOT REPORTED. (302/JMQ)***AFTER HEARING, AN ORDER IS SIGNED.

calendar.matters:
Notice Of Hearing On First Amended Petition For Approval Of Transfer Of Structured Settlement Payment Rights
Off Calendar May-09-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: RE: PETITIONER TBT SERSVICES, LLC'S Notice Of Hearing On First Amended Petition For Approval Of Transfer Of Structured Settlement Payment Rights.

Appearances:

Martin Shives, Esq., of SUMMERS & SHIVES, A.P.C.., via Zoom videoconference, for Berkshire Hathaway Life Insurance Company of Nebraska, specially appearing interested Party.

John Z. Shafai, Esq., Laww Offices of, (310) 466-4282, for TBT Services, LLC, Petitioner.

Arleen Salonga, personal appearance, in propria persona, interested Party/Payee.

The matter is argued. Ms. Salonga offers brief argument. At the conclusion of argument, the court adopts its tentative ruling.

Petitioner TBT Services, LLC's First Amended Petition For Approval Of Transfer Of Structured Settlement Payment Rights is DENIED.

Petitioner filed the petition anonymizing Respondent and did so without an authorizing court order; nothing in the record supports the action.

Petitioner provided all the information required under Insurance Code section 10139.5(f)(1). For example, Petitioner did not provide the original annuity contract or the underlying structured settlement agreement.

Petitioner has not established the transfer is in Respondent's best interest. (See Insurance Code section 10139.5(a)(1).) It appears Petitioner negotiated with Respondent while Respondent was still a minor and did not have the capacity to enter a legally binding contract. This timing also suggests that any efforts to get meaningful quotes or offers from other potential purchasers were quite limited. The court would need much more information regarding these efforts, including documentation regarding competing quotes and offers.

The court is sympathetic towards Respondent's immediate financial needs, but these are they types of financial needs generally considered at the time a minor's compromise involving a structured settlement is reviewed and they are factors in the court's decision to approve or reject a proposed structured settlement; here, the federal court factored such circumstances and determined the current structured settlement was appropriate and would serve Respondent's long-term interests. To approve this transfer, the court would need to know more about Respondent's current situation, including her current living situation, and her future plans. Again, though, the court does not reach these issues on this petition because it is fundamentally flawed in other ways.

The court would need more information regarding Respondent's refusal to obtain independent professional advice regarding the proposed transfer, especially give TBT would be paying the fee up to $1,500. If the other material infirmities were not present, the court would like order Respondent to secure independent professional advice regarding the proposed transfer, at least until the $1,500 fee cap is reached. (See Insurance Code section 10139.5(b)(14).)

Petitioner misstates the relevant ADR, which is, in fact, over 24.5%. On this record, the court, which has substantial experience in this area, cannot find a 24.5% ADR reasonable, particularly in light of the BIFCO Hardship Exchange Program Option involving an ADR below 7% set out in Berkshire Hathaway Life Insurance Company of Nebraska's Response to the First Amended Petition, filed in this action on April 30, 2025.

The court orders the following, in open court: should a new petition be filed, it shall be preceded by the interested Party/Payee meeting with an independent financial advisor. See final paragraph of the court's tentative ruling.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Not Reported. (302/JMQ)

***After hearing, an order is signed.
